I own about a dozen Casio digital watches (I'm kind of a collector) and this one is tied with my G-Shock DW-5600E as being my favorite.  Casio makes and sells tons of F-91 watches for a reason, and have done so for a long time. The beauty of this watch is that it is so light and thin that it almost becomes a part of your body and you forget that it is on your wrist. At the same time the F-91 is quite accurate, reliable, and sturdy considering it's super low cost. Even if it does break, which would require significant abuse, the low price means getting a new one is painless. The batteries on these things will last a good long time. As far as accuracy, my F-91 actually does much better than my DW-5600E. The style isn't for everyone but I personally love the retro look. The only down side is that you don't want to be caught in a warzone wearing one because ownership of an F-91 could get you accused of being a terrorist bomber and sent to Gitmo. That aside, for anyone who wants an inexpensive and / or retro style watch the F-91 is the clear choice.

title_review: Lightweight and nice looking, but didn't fit me... also, the price...?
text:
I like these glasses for the weight and appearance, and for the gradient lenses, which are great for driving on an average sunny day. They are not terribly dark, however, which means you can wear them even in lower light conditions like dusk or dawn, or when there is just a glary thin cloud layer, but if I was doing anything around snow or water or was going to be out in bright sun for an extended period, I would want something darker.

The thing that didn't work for me was the long earpieces. They were too long for me, bending down a good half-inch or more behind my ears and thus did not hold them in place, allowing them to slide down on me. My fault for not realizing the 150mm arms would be too long - I guess I'm more in the 140 to 145 range - but they don't work.

Beyond that, the price is a bit high, I think. If I was buying these at a Walgreens or Walmart and trying them on, any price tag above about $25 would have had me taking a pass. They just don't feel any better in quality or construction that sunglasses in that lower range - certainly not of the caliber of other sunglasses I've bought in the $40 - $60 price range.

title_review: Outside looks fine. Quality control lacking on the inside.
text: At the end of the manufacturing line this particular shirt should have ended up in a pile of factory seconds. On the inside of the shirt there was a long string of the material used as reinforcing at the neck. One end of that string was sewn into the neck and the other end was sewn into the bottom hem. I got tangled in it when I first tried to put the shirt on. I cut it off. In one armpit there is a snarled mess of the thread used to sew the panels together. I teased that clump loose enough to find the loop, cut the loop in the middle and tied the ends together. Don't know how long this shirt will stay in one piece. As I said, it's definitely a factory second. Too bad I paid full price. On the bright side, before ordering, I went to the Russell website to consult the sizing chart. My dimensions are at the very upper end of the range for size medium so I picked medium and it fits quite well.

title_review: The Best orthotic support shoes I've ever worn.
text:
I had PF problems last winter in Florida.
My Podiatrist there sent me to a High End running shoe store and I Bought a $150.00 pair of Hoka running shoes.
They provided good support for most of a day. With Occupational Health Therapy my feet improved.
I believe that my Hoka's were a half size too big after the swelling reduced. They are still comfortable but after a half day of wear they start hurting my feet by allowing too much pronation. Plus they bothered the Gout that I developed.

The we returned to Massachusetts and my foot problems started to return with the addition of Gout.
I looked online (Amazon) for a slip on shoe that would help my PF and diabetic bunion issues, to take pressure off of the major Joint on my left foot.

I found these "Ortho Comfort Loafers" (PF supoort and Diabetic Bunion relief) slip ons and after seeral weeks of wear every day, all day they are the most Effective and Comfortable Orthotic shoes I've ever worn.

They aren't good for standing still for extended periods, but my Podiatrists tell me that My PF will reassert itself if I am no "striding so as to roll my feet forward from heel to toe." When I stride (instead of shuffle) my PF pain goes away.

Best orthotic shoe I've ever worn.

The material is awesome, though probably more for either over a bra or under a shirt, as the fabric is quite sheer. The functionality is as expected  - works well with the limtied functions available and if you want a good looking/retro inexpensive watch that is light and durable you are in luck. title_review: Hands down the best value watch of all time.
text:
I'm into watches and have a collection of about a dozen which range from Swiss automatics to digitals like this one. I have had amazing luck these Casios in terms of reliability and water resistance. I've worn these everywhere from the ocean to the shower and never had an issue. I wouldn't necessarily recommend that someone do that, but I've never had a leak. This watch has all the features you need with a cool retro look to match.

I've owned a few rectangular G Shock watches as well. I prefer the understated looks of this F91W, however, the G Shocks in the $40 range have a few noticeable upgrades. For one, I have turned the alarm on my F91W a few times when I've fallen asleep wearing it. The issue is there are no guards around the buttons, so they are fairly easy to push accidentally. The other is the lights on G Shocks have been better in my experience.

Overall this watch is extremely easy to recommend for a feature rich, simple, inexpensive digital watch.

I am a senior woman, tall with long leg bones from my knee to my ankle. I developed chemo-induced peripheral neuropathy after cancer treatment and have been struggling with it since then. I cannot stand a close chew on and have been forced to wear flip-flops since my treatment. I finally found these compression socks with no solid foot in them. I used to ride horses when I was younger and developed rather large calf muscles so when I went to order them I ordered the largest size available which was a 3XL. My first pair was in black and I was very pleased with how they worked; I could get them on and off fairly easy without too much effort. So I ordered a pair in nude and when they came they were so much shorter in the foot area and narrower in the calf and Ankle area that I sent them back thinking it was marked with a wrong size or that the color somehow affected the sizing. So I turned around and ordered a pair in white and when it came in they were also shorter/narrower so I thought maybe it was me . I pulled them on with much difficulty this time and wore them but I could barely get them off because my hands didn't have the strength to roll them down and pull them off. Not sure if this is true for all sizes or people with smaller calves but for me I guess I'm stuck wearing the original black ones that fit so well when I got them.

title_review: Not as Dark as They Appear to Be
text:
These KKP aviator sunglasses are probably the most comfortable pair of sunglasses I have at the moment. At least for the moment, they are exactly tight enough to stay in place without causing me any discomfort around the ear or nose pieces. That's a huge plus, and I attribute that to the glasses being both very well-constructed and very lightweight, a nice combination.

My only problem with these - and it's a big problem for me - is that the lenses allow considerably more light to reach my eyes than I expect a pair of sunglasses to let in. These are one of the very few sunglasses that I actually find myself still squinting a bit while wearing them during the middle of the day. I actually reached up at one point to make sure that I was wearing them (that's how comfortable they can be) after realizing that I was having to squint while walking toward the sun. I suppose the good news is that they can be worn safely later in the day while driving - I like to use polarized lenses like these to help me pick up more detail while driving. So there is a bit of a trade-off there.

The sunglasses do seem to be a little expensive, all things considered, but if they eventually prove to be more durable than average (something only time will tell) that would make them worth the higher cost.

Bottom Line: These are for those who prefer a little more light reaching their eyes while still getting the other benefits of wearing sunglasses.

title_review: Two pairs - two very different results
text:
I ordered two pairs of these sweatpants on the same day. Same size. Got two very different products, so I guess I'm doing two different reviews (edit: I cannot submit two different reviews, so I guess it will be a two-parter).

This is for the navy blue pair:

First, the fit is weird. They are snug through the hips and thighs, but extra long. I have long legs, so I can wear them, but if you need a shorter inseam, they probably won't work.

Second, the material is horrible. Really rough - like burlap. I washed them and used fabric softener hoping it was just sizing or something, but it isn't.

I guess I will use these to work outdoors. They definitely aren't the comfy lounge pants I was hoping they were.

Now for the mauve pair:

Excellent fit, buttery soft material. I love these pants! Hard to believe they were sold in the same listing as the blue ones. I guess I will add a star since I can't review them separately. Whoever is doing quality control on this product needs to step it up a bit.

title_review: Difficult to size but soft and stretchy
text:
Iâ€™ve gotten these in several different sizes and styles now, trying to find one that would actually work best for us. They are available in full sock, toe-less, and leg sleeve and we have tried all three.
My first complaint is that they are definitely made for people with longer legs. The size in the description does say for leg lengths 14 to 17â€, and you will definitely be most comfortable on the longer side of that - although with the amount of stretch in these, you can fold down the top if needed (our measurement are 20â€ calf when not swollen, and 14â€ from heel to the top of the calf and we definitely had over 3â€ of fold down at the top on the 4x). They are also made for a larger foot size, so if your foot is average womenâ€™s range (8.5 to 9 womenâ€™s), you will find the foot bed to be a bit long - this does leave plenty of toe room, so that might be a plus for you. The material is soft and very thick, and we have not been able to test them yet in warm circumstances since we have fairly cool weather where I am right now.
The description lists a 3X calf to be 20 to 26 inch circumference, and a 4X calf to be 20 to 28 inch circumference, thatâ€™s a HUGE overlapping range, so you can see how this would leave a lot of confusion as to which size you might need. And there is a big difference in the feel between the 3X and the 4x. The 3X is very snug, and feels like it would provide a good bit of compression. The 4X were very comfortable (even with the 3 1/2â€ fold down), so itâ€™s hard to say if they would actually provide any/enough compression when the leg is swollen. The 4X in the toe-less was even roomier at the top of the calf then in the full-foot version.
I think our favorite out of all of them was using the calf sleeve and actually bringing it down over the heel to make it into a toe-less. That took care of the problem of a too large foot bed and a too long calf length.
So I feel these are decent but Iâ€™ve definitely had much better compression products. They most certainly need a much better sizing chart easily accessible, maybe in the photo section. And I feel like they definitely need to make a version for people with shorter legs. I did not notice any unpleasant odor in any of the versions we tried, which Iâ€™d read from another reviewer was an issue. The price point of $27 is definitely on the high end, I would expect you should get two for that price.

title_review: High Quality with Very Good Arch Support & Cushioning.
text:
I'm very pleased with the high quality construction, fit, support, and overall comfort with one small caveat.
That minor criticism is the little bumps on the insole that are claimed to massage the foot. These bumps can give the feeling of sand or grit in your shoe, and while they may improve air circulation, they certainly don't massage because your foot is stationary in the shoe.

There is another insole insert without the little bumps, but that doesn't have much of any arch support, which I need.

After several days of wearing, the bumps have become less noticeable, but I point it out because I would have preferred a smooth insole surface, and I don't understand their rationale in an otherwise very well designed and constructed shoe.

I didn't subtract a star because I have some neuropathy in my feet from spinal stenosis, and that may be contributing to my slightly adverse sensation, which might not be the case for you.

title_review: Decent sunglasses, but not sure if worth the price.
text:
I was back and forth on giving these a 3 or 4 star review.
Pros: All around, these sunglasses are bigger than any pair I've ever really bought with this kind of plastic frame. I have a large head, and it is good to finally have a pair of sunglasses that I don't have to rely on streching out before they are finally comfortable. I can imagine this would be a con to someone who has a smaller head though. The frames are oversized and I love it. They have a fancy-walk down Times Square kind of feel, but again, I have a large head and large face. I know that someone with a smaller head would get taken over by these sunglasses. The large lens also is nice because it gives more of a chance to block out the sun; however, that leads me to the list of cons.
Cons: Firstly, in the advertisement picture, there is a small little white writing across the top of one of the lenses. I was expecting this to be almost unreadable due to how small it looks in the picture; however, it is very easy to see in real life. I saw it when I was taking a selfie and had to take them off because I couldn't figure out what was on my glasses. Additionally, the picture description says these are polarized, and I don't know how to test it for sure, but I do not think these are polarized. It felt like I just had a black screen over my eyes. I didn't really feel any protection or polarization from them.

So for those reasons, I decided to give it three stars because I don't think they are worth the price. They have a strong plastic, and they have a great style, but I don't think they are what they are advertised. If they were cheaper, I would probably recommend, but for this price, I don't think I would.

title_review: Stylish & Comfortable But Better Values Elsewhere
text:
These glasses are very stylish - the way that the lens is attached in front of the frame gives them a unique and fashionable look. The metal portion of the frame contrasts nicely with the dark frame on the "Tan" color glasses that I evaluated. They also occur as "big," from a fashion sense, so if big lenses look good on your face, you'll be pleased. ...And they're lightweight and comfortable.
I took a star away because the lenses aren't polarized as advertised. You can easily check for polarized lenses by holding your Sunglasses up to a computer screen, then rotate 90 degrees. If your lenses become dark or you can no longer see through them, they are polarized. Note you can also test in the same way by rotating the lens in question while looking through another pair of polarized lenses.
I took two more stars away because the hinge is not spring loaded, and the price seems high. Comparatively speaking, I can get Prescription, polarized sunglasses with spring hinges for about $30 from companies like Zenni Optical.

title_review: One of the best watches ever made regardless of price
text: I own several watches but this is the one I go to most often. It has just about everything you need in a watch (accurate time, the date, a stop watch, and an alarm) while being so small and light that you forget you're wearing it. Even if you spent tens of thousands of dollars on a different watch, it is unlikely that it would be anywhere near as comfortable as this one. What people say about the light is mostly true. It isn't very bright. That said, it is still bright enough to read the time when it's dark. There are times when a super bright light is not preferred, especially when your eyes are light sensitive because you've been sleeping or have been in a dark area for a while.
